protocol.note: FROZEN at protocolMajor=1 (M3 contract freeze, R-CLI-004) — the surface is stable and changes only through the R-CLI-010 deprecation lifecycle.

eventTopics.name: files
eventTopics.description: Post-derivation file change facts (never raw filesystem noise).

fields.name: path
fields.description: The project-relative file the fact is about.

fields.name: change
fields.description: The change class: added | modified | removed.

eventTopics.name: derivation
eventTopics.description: Derived-world updates, each stamped with the input content-hash.

fields.name: event
fields.description: The derivation event, e.g. derivation.settled.

fields.name: generation
fields.description: The derived-world generation this update reflects.

fields.name: inputHash
fields.description: The content-hash of the input file version derived from (the write-acknowledgement).

eventTopics.name: diagnostics
eventTopics.description: Machine-readable diagnostics through the R-CLI-008 error schema.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The R-CLI-008 catalog error code.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The human-readable diagnostic message.

fields.name: stability
fields.description: provisional while the generation churns; promoted to stable once it settles (R-BRIDGE-008).

fields.name: pointer
fields.description: Optional RFC 6901 JSON-pointer / location the diagnostic is about.

fields.name: event
fields.description: The lifecycle event, e.g. started | reloaded | stopped.

fields.name: event
fields.description: attached | detached.

fields.name: protocolMajor
fields.description: The negotiated protocol major (on attach).

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: The granted scope names (on attach).

eventTopics.name: log
eventTopics.description: Structured log entries (severity, source, tick, session).

fields.name: level
fields.description: The severity: trace | debug | info | warn | error.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The log message text.

fields.name: source
fields.description: The origin: ts | native | module (R-BRIDGE-008).

fields.name: tick
fields.description: The sim tick, when the entry is session-scoped.

fields.name: session
fields.description: The session id, when session-scoped.

fields.name: seq
fields.description: The monotonic, totally-ordered event seq within the incarnation (R-BRIDGE-008).

fields.name: incarnationId
fields.description: The daemon incarnation epoch; a restart forces a fresh snapshot rather than a stale since-seq resume.

fields.name: generation
fields.description: The derived-world generation the event reflects.

fields.name: topic
fields.description: The event topic name.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: The topic-specific payload (see eventTopics).

errorCatalog.message: The command could not be parsed against the verb grammar.
No such verb in the contract surface.
Unknown flag for this verb.
A required argument was not supplied.
A package's reserved namespace collides with an already-registered one.
The referenced file does not exist.
The file is not well-formed and could not be parsed.
The payload failed schema validation for its kind.
The --if-match hash did not match the file's current bytes.
The path escapes the project root and was refused.
The client protocol major is outside the daemon's compatibility window.
Engine/protocol versions are incompatible; attach refused.
The package declares an engine-compat range the running engine does not satisfy.
The payload is stamped for a newer engine schema; last-good state retained.
The payload is stamped for a newer package schema; last-good state retained.
The verb surface is reserved in the contract but its backing is not wired in this version.
An unexpected internal error occurred.
The attach token's scope does not permit this method (least privilege, R-SEC-007).
A structural three-way merge left an unresolved field conflict; see the conflict envelope.
The same intra-file id was added on both sides relative to base — a structural conflict, never silently unified (L-33).
A binary sidecar differs on both sides; binaries are never content-merged — resolve whole-file ours/theirs (L-33).
Both sides minted a different GUID for the same asset meta; GUID identity is never field-blended — resolve whole-asset ours/theirs (L-36).
A merge input carries payloads stamped newer than the installed schemas; a whole-file conflict class, never a parse error (L-37).
Two objects in one file share an intra-file id (an id add/add or a raw copy); re-key one via `context re-key`. The post-merge convergence gate.
`context resolve-conflict` named a --path with no open conflict in the merge sidecar.
The re-key target does not resolve to an object carrying a stable intra-file id (or the requested id is invalid).
The attach token holds a lower scope than this method requires; re-attach with the needed scope.
This verb is operational: it is served by a live daemon over RPC (attach to a running 'context daemon'), not as a one-shot CLI verb.
The resource handle is unknown to this daemon instance (expired, foreign, or malformed).
No registered migration step covers a version in the payload's chain (a gap).
A migration step reported failure; the document rolled back.
A payload exceeded the per-invocation migration budget (L-37); the document rolled back.
A migration step altered, moved, added, or removed an id/guid — identity is immutable (L-37); the document rolled back.
Package-shipped migrations execute only in the sandboxed WASM tier; the VM component is not stood up yet (L-37 contract; execution deliberately stubbed).
An override path has no destination after migration; the entry is preserved but excluded from flatten (L-37 orphan override).
The file does not begin with the sidecar magic.
The sidecar file is shorter than its fixed header.
The sidecar header carries a format version this engine cannot read; last-good state retained.
A $sidecar reference is not the canonical {"$sidecar": "<relpath>", "hash": "<decimal>"} shape.
A $sidecar reference names a sidecar file that does not exist.
A sidecar file exists with no referencing owner.
The sidecar's whole-file raw bytes do not hash to the referencing "hash" value.
Two live assets claim the same GUID (raw copy?); the lexicographically-first path keeps it — re-key the duplicate via `context validate --fix`.
A meta sidecar's asset file is missing (raw move or delete); healing pairs unique moves, `context validate --fix` cleans deliberate deletes.
A meta sidecar is malformed (not well-formed JSON, root not an object, or no valid "guid"); non-canonical formatting alone is tolerated.
Raw-move healing found no UNIQUE orphan/newcomer pairing; nothing was written — re-run the move via `context asset move` or resolve by hand.
A reference resolves to no indexed asset (unknown $ref GUID, or a path that names nothing).
A path-only reference (accepted, L-34); `context validate --fix` or the next tool save resolves the authoritative $ref GUID.
A dual-form reference's path hint no longer matches the asset's location; healed on tool save (L-34).
The move/rename source asset does not exist.
The move/rename destination is occupied by a different asset (or an orphaned sidecar holding one's identity); move never overwrites.
The move/rename request is malformed (a sidecar, temp-residue, or dot-tree path, or an empty path).
The source asset is not the format its extension claims, or its container is truncated / structurally invalid; nothing was imported.
The source asset's container parsed but its contents could not be decoded (e.g. a chunk CRC mismatch or an unreadable payload).
The source asset uses a format variant this importer does not support in v1 (e.g. a non-PCM WAV or a non-2.0 glTF); it is refused, never silently mis-imported.
An importer run attempted to read or write outside its TOCTOU-safe path jail, or requested the denied network capability; the run was refused (R-SEC-006/008/010).

The isolated importer subprocess failed to spawn, was killed by its per-OS sandbox primitive (seccomp-bpf and friends), or exited without returning a result; nothing was imported and the run fails closed (R-SEC-006).
An importer produced different bytes across the CI double-run byte-compare; the shared cache is only sound for run-deterministic importers, so this fails the gate.
A shared-cache entry failed its content-hash self-verification on read (corruption); the entry is rejected and the artifact re-derived (R-FILE-010).
The composed-write target does not resolve — the id-path names no composed entity, or an --at-instance prefix names no instancing scene.
The field pointer addresses an identity field (/id, /$schema, /version) that is immutable under composition (L-37).
A tilemap chunk's packed cell payload exceeds the ~1 MB split-nudge ceiling; split the region (L-33 advisory).
A tilemap chunk region has a non-positive width or height.
Two tilemap tile-sets or two layers share a stable id (L-33 ids are unique within a collection).
Two string-table locales declare the same tag.
Two string-table entries declare the same key.
A locale's fallback names a locale not declared in the table's `locales`.
A locale's fallback chain contains a cycle.
A string-table translation is not EXACTLY ONE of `text` or `plural`.
A plural set omits the required CLDR `other` category.
Two translations for one string-table key declare the same locale.
The save document shape is invalid (not a save envelope, a bad composed identity, or a component payload the save header does not stamp).
The save carries a component this build's compiled component set does not include; a save migration runner embeds migrations for exactly the compiled set (R-DATA-005).
A saved component payload is stamped more schema versions behind than the declared save back-compat scope (N versions) covers; refused, never best-effort read (R-DATA-005).
The save envelope's format version is newer than this build reads; last-good retained, never a best-effort parse (R-DATA-005).
The session-state document is malformed, an unsupported version, or names ids that are not restorable.
The named session-state file does not exist.
A synthetic input event / action activation injection was malformed (unknown kind or a missing field).
The replay artifact is malformed or an unsupported version.
The project inputs drifted from the replay artifact's content manifest; reported as drift BEFORE running, never a silent divergence.
A deterministic replay diverged from its expected per-tick hash trace; the first divergent tick is reported.
The query expression could not be parsed against the R-CLI-012 grammar; see the byte offset in the diagnostic pointer.
The query used an operator or predicate function not in the enumerated R-CLI-012 operator set (equality / range / existence / string-match).
The pagination cursor is malformed, from a foreign daemon incarnation, or not the unified R-CLI-012 / R-BRIDGE-008 cursor shape; re-issue the query without a cursor.
The query named a surface other than the derived world, live-sim state, or schema introspection — the one language spans exactly those three.
TypeScript could not be transpiled to JavaScript (a syntax or transform error); see the diagnostic text.
The TypeScript entrypoint could not be bundled (an unresolved import or transform error); see the diagnostic text.
TypeScript failed a semantic typecheck (a --noEmit type error); the diagnostic carries the tsc code (TSxxxx) and the authored .ts line:column.
Authored TypeScript threw at runtime; the diagnostic carries a TS-source-mapped stack trace (authored .ts position, not the transpiled JS position).
The subscription id is not a live subscription on this daemon incarnation (never subscribed, already unsubscribed, or from a prior incarnation); re-subscribe.
An engine-driven install requires an exact pinned version; a dependency spec is a range, dist-tag, or url (R-SEC-005). The install is refused, never floated.

A fetched package artifact's bytes did not match its lockfile integrity (SRI) hash, or the SRI named no verifiable algorithm; the artifact is refused, never used with a warning (verify-before-use, fail-closed — R-SEC-009).
A dependency is missing from the lockfile, or a lock entry lacks an exact version / integrity — the dependency graph is not fully pinned (incl. transitive), so the install is refused fail-closed.

The package declares an install lifecycle script, classifying it native-tier; engine-driven installs never run lifecycle scripts (--ignore-scripts, all tiers), so it is refused pending the L-49 consent gate (see consent_required).

The package source could not supply a pinned artifact's bytes (e.g. the offline --source cache lacks the tarball) — a source/cache miss, distinct from a lockfile-completeness or integrity defect. The install is refused fail-closed.

A native-tier / build+install action was requested without the needed grant; it returns this machine-readable code (carrying the requested scope + an approval ref) and resumes the same idempotency-keyed op once granted out-of-band (R-SEC-011).
Attaching the V8 in-box CDP inspector session failed (the inspector could not be created or connected through EditorKernel); no debug session was established.
This build has no V8 backend, so no CDP inspector can be attached; the debug attach is available only where the V8 host is linked (the CI/MSVC-tier builds).
A stable intra-file id is not the L-33 form (16..32 lowercase hex chars); re-key it via `context re-key`. The `context validate` FORMAT gate, sibling to merge.duplicate_id.
No GPU adapter is available to render the observer viewport; absence is reported, never a fabricated frame (R-HEAD-002).
The L-41 CEF compositing surface for the observer viewport could not be acquired (e.g. the selected mode needs a GPU shared-texture surface but no GPU compositor is present).
The observer viewport's scene render or pixel readback failed (R-REND-002).
A play control (pause / step / hot-reload) was issued with no live play session; start play first (L-51 edit state).
The play session could not be started over the edit state; nothing was played and no authored file was written (L-51 fail-closed).
Advancing the running play session by a fixed tick failed; the session state is unchanged (R-SIM-002 fail-closed).
A live authored edit could not be reflected into the running play session (L-22 hot reload); the running session is unchanged.
A forbidden relaxed-FP flag (fast-math) reached the sim path of a deterministic build; deterministic:true is refused, never forged (R-SIM-005).
A deterministic build did not have the strict floating-point model in effect (MSVC /fp:strict); the produced attestation fails closed rather than claim unverified determinism.
A deterministic build was requested but recorded no applied strict-FP flags to attest over; the attestation cannot be PRODUCED from verified flags, so it is refused (R-SEC-009 fail-closed).
A dead or null entity handle was passed to a physics operation; nothing was simulated or modified (fail-closed).
A physics operation targeted an entity that lacks the full physics component set (transform + velocity + body + collider); the world is unchanged.
A collider was rejected: a sphere radius or box half-extent was not positive; no physics components were added (fail-closed validation).
A dynamic body was rejected: its mass was not positive; no physics components were added (fail-closed validation).
A physics simulation step was refused: the fixed tick duration was not positive; the world is unchanged.
A dead or null entity handle was passed to a physics operation; nothing was simulated or modified (fail-closed).
A physics operation targeted an entity that lacks the full physics component set (transform + velocity + body + collider); the world is unchanged.
A collider was rejected: a circle radius or box half-extent was not positive; no physics components were added (fail-closed validation).
A dynamic body was rejected: its mass was not positive; no physics components were added (fail-closed validation).
A physics simulation step was refused: the fixed tick duration was not positive; the world is unchanged.
A dead or null entity handle was passed to an animation operation; nothing was simulated or modified (fail-closed).
An animation operation targeted an entity that lacks the animator component; the world is unchanged.

A rig was rejected: it has no clips or graph states, an out-of-range initial state, a non-positive clip duration, or a graph state / transition naming a non-existent clip or state; the AnimationWorld keeps its previous rig (fail-closed validation).
An animator could not be attached: the entity already carries one; nothing was overwritten (fail-closed validation).
An animation simulation step was refused: the fixed tick duration was not positive; the world is unchanged.
A dead or null entity handle was passed to a particle operation; nothing was simulated or modified (fail-closed).
A particle operation targeted an entity that lacks the emitter component; the world is unchanged.
An emitter description was rejected: a negative emission rate, a non-positive particle lifetime, or a negative velocity spread; no component was added (fail-closed validation).
A particle simulation step was refused: the fixed tick duration was not positive; the world is unchanged.
A dead or null entity handle was passed to a spline operation; nothing was simulated or modified (fail-closed).
A spline operation targeted an entity that lacks the path-follower component; the world is unchanged.

A path selection was rejected: the installed curve set is empty or malformed, or a follower named an out-of-range path index; no follower is attached and the world keeps its prior paths (fail-closed validation).
A path follower could not be attached: the entity already carries one; nothing was overwritten (fail-closed validation).
A spline simulation step was refused: the fixed tick duration was not positive; the world is unchanged.
A dead or null entity handle was passed to an audio observe/spatialize operation; nothing was read or triggered (fail-closed).
An audio mixing-bus graph was rejected: it is empty, has a duplicate bus id, or a bus names a non-existent or cyclic parent; no bus graph is installed (fail-closed validation).
An audio event was rejected: a negative gain, an inverted/degenerate spatialization range, or a reference to an out-of-range bus; nothing was triggered (fail-closed validation).
The audio device could not be initialized; audio playback is disabled. The simulation is unaffected — audio is a presentation observer off the sim path (fail-closed for audio only).
An input context was rejected: an empty context id, or a binding with an empty device/code/action or an unrecognized device source; no context was installed (fail-closed validation).
An input context could not be installed: its id is already installed; nothing was overwritten (fail-closed validation).
An input operation named a context id that is not installed (or popped an empty active stack); the active stack is unchanged.
A rebind named an action that has no binding in the target context; nothing was repointed (fail-closed).
A JS-tier GC-discipline or GC-profiler operation needs the in-process JS VM, but this build carries the stub backend; nothing ran (fail-closed — the simulation is unaffected).
A scheduled inter-tick GC window was refused: the requested pause budget is not a finite positive duration; nothing was collected (fail-closed validation).
The JS VM reported a failure while running a scheduled inter-tick GC window or a GC-profiler query; the simulation state is unaffected (GC touches the JS heap only).
A replication registration used the unassigned network identity (net id 0); nothing was registered (fail-closed validation).
Two entities were registered for replication with the same network identity; the second was refused so the composed-id keyed mapping stays 1:1 (fail-closed validation).

A state-sync snapshot carried a component payload whose byte length disagrees with the replicated component set's declared size for that component; nothing was applied and the replica is unchanged (fail-closed validation).
A state-sync delta targeted an entity the replica itself holds authority over; the authoritative peer's local state wins, so the delta is refused and the replica is unchanged.
The named UI-scene file does not exist.
The UI-scene document is malformed, an unsupported version, or names an unknown role or event kind; nothing was built (fail-closed validation).
A UI drive/assert verb named a node (by author name) that is not present in the tree.
A `ui send` request was malformed: an unknown event kind, or a required field for it (target node, key code, or text) was missing; nothing was dispatched (fail-closed).
A `ui assert` expectation did not hold over the loaded tree; the diagnostic reports the asserted fact, the expected value, and the actual value (the headless-CLI assert verdict).
The per-platform transcode table has no row for this (artifact kind, platform); the variant is surfaced as unavailable, never guessed (R-BUILD-003).
The transcode table named a target engine format this encoder cannot produce (e.g. an uncompressed row); a table gap, not a malformed source asset.
A described-kind artifact could not be transcoded because its bytes are not a well-formed descriptor; nothing was produced (fail-closed).

The runnable project/template failed pre-build verification (a missing, malformed, or empty root scene, or a blocking composition diagnostic); OR the R-BUILD-004 export template (the shipped --runtime host binary) failed verify-before-use against the pinned trust root (R-SEC-009 / L-58 — a tampered, unsigned, or untrusted-key signature). Nothing was built/packaged (fail-closed validation).

The per-target toolchain manifest (R-PKG-002 / L-42) could not supply the requested target's toolchain (no manifest entry for the target); OR an engine-fetched toolchain artifact failed verify-before-use against the pinned trust root (R-SEC-009 — an unverifiable fetch is a failed fetch). The toolchain cannot be fetched/trusted. Transient — a re-fetch against a repaired manifest or a correctly-signed artifact can succeed.
The authored-script (TypeScript) AOT tier could not be produced for the target — a malformed or unresolvable script entrypoint; the build is refused fail-closed (deterministic).
A per-platform asset transcode node failed while producing the target's variant (the diagnostic carries the a03 transcode.* detail code); nothing was packed (fail-closed, deterministic).

The final-link path failed: the R-KERNEL-003 generated-registration TU references a package with no registrable module (an undefined register_<pkg> — the link's undefined-symbol failure). Deterministic — a bare retry cannot conjure the missing module.

One or more required toolchain components for a requested target are missing or the wrong version; `context doctor` refuses (the environment cannot build the requested target(s)). The report enumerates each finding with its fetchable-vs-preinstalled remediation (fail-closed).

A required toolchain component is absent for a requested target; the finding's `fetchable` flag says whether the fix is an engine-fetch (via the a08-verified path, R-SEC-009) or a dev-preinstalled prerequisite. Deterministic — a bare re-run cannot conjure the component.

A required toolchain component is present but its version does not satisfy the L-42 manifest pin; blocking only when the pin's enforcement is `strict` (an advisory/documented drift is a non-blocking warning — the drift-alarm, not a build blocker).

The per-user file-sync watch budget is below the project file count × worktree-daemon count (the up-front R-FILE-002 check). ADVISORY: raise the limit, or expect the watcher.degraded background-crawl fallback. Pairs with the R-FILE-011 N-daemons-on-one-box scenario.

A code-signing prerequisite for a requested target (Windows Authenticode identity, macOS signing identity + notary creds) is not configured/reachable. ADVISORY (a ship-time prereq, never a build blocker). Presence only — the doctor never surfaces a secret/key value.
`context doctor --target <t>` named a value that is not a known build target (windows | linux | macos | web).

A produced artifact for a target that REQUIRES code-signing (Windows Authenticode, R-SEC-003) carries no signature — an advisory, never-silent WARNING folded into the build SUCCESS envelope's data.signing (e.g. a fork PR with no signing secrets). The build still succeeded; the artifact must be Authenticode-signed (Azure Trusted Signing, or the developer-certificate fallback, with a mandatory RFC-3161 timestamp) before shipping. Deterministic.
The addressed tilemap layer id exists in no `layers` entry of the document (layers are addressed by their L-33 stable id, never by index or name).
A cell edit lies inside no chunk `region` of the addressed layer — v1 authoring rewrites EXISTING chunks and never invents new ones (the chunk topology is the M2 kind's).
A tile id falls in no tile-set's [firstTileId, firstTileId + tileCount) global range (tile 0 = empty is always valid — an erase is a paint with 0).
The attach token is missing or does not match the daemon's instance token; the attach is refused (attach-token enforcement, D20 / R-SEC-002).
The daemon is already serving its maximum number of concurrent clients; the attach is refused. Transient — a slot frees when a client detaches, so a later retry can succeed.

largeResult.envelopeField: an oversized success result is replaced by data.largeResult carrying the handle shape

handleShape.handle: string — the opaque resource URI; treat as opaque and feed back to resource.read verbatim
handleShape.sizeBytes: number — total payload size in bytes
handleShape.localPath: string? — same-filesystem fast-path hint (optional; an optimization, never the sole mechanism)

largeResult.readParams: { handle: string, range?: { offsetBytes: number, lengthBytes: number } }

readResultShape.handle: string — the URI that was read
readResultShape.lengthBytes: number — decoded bytes in this chunk
readResultShape.chunkHex: string — the chunk bytes, lowercase hex-encoded (binary-clean inside the JSON envelope)

ordering.rule: results carry a mandatory TOTAL order; the sort always ends with an '@id asc' tiebreaker (the entity id is unique), so paginated results never reorder across pages. Missing keys sort last.

shape.incarnationId: string — the daemon incarnation; a restart invalidates the cursor (forces a fresh snapshot), shared with R-BRIDGE-008
shape.seq: number — the R-BRIDGE-008 monotonic, totally-ordered event seq (event catch-up position)
shape.generation: number — the derived-world generation this page is consistent to (every page of one paged query reads one generation)
shape.after: string — keyset position: the last entity id returned under the total order; empty for an event cursor

cursor.reconciliation: ONE cursor model, not two: {incarnationId, seq} is the R-BRIDGE-008 event cursor verbatim; an event cursor is a query cursor with an empty 'after'. Query paging adds generation + after for mutation-stable keyset paging (R-CLI-017: a handle carries one oversized payload, a cursor a paged sequence).

stringSemantics.caseHandling: explicit per-operator: comparisons are case-SENSITIVE by default; the i-prefixed string-match forms (icontains/istartswith/iendswith/imatches) are case-INSENSITIVE
stringSemantics.v1Scope: strict UTF-8 validation on every literal; ASCII is invariant under NFC and passes through; ASCII case-folding backs the i-forms. The full Unicode canonical decomposition+composition table and non-ASCII case-folding are the tracked follow-up — the SEMANTICS are contract now, so activating the full table is non-breaking.

queryLanguage.composesWith: mutation verbs: `context query … | context set …` (the query result's entity ids + provenance feed a following mutation), R-CLI-006

fields.description: The M1 placeholder kind marker; the $schema header is authoritative.
Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
Scene composition (L-35): each entry instances another scene under a stable intra-file id; per-instance state lives in `overrides`.
Stable intra-file id of this instance — the first segment of override id-paths addressing into its subtree (L-33/L-35).
Project-root-relative path of the instanced scene (becomes a typed x-ctx-ref when the asset database lands, L-34/L-36).
Schema-blessed annotations.

Id-path-addressed override entries ([instanceId, ..., entityId] — L-35), innermost-out precedence (the OUTERMOST instancing scene wins). Exactly one kind per entry: `pointer`+`value` (per-field), `add` (structural add), or `remove: true` (structural remove) — enforced by the composition model.
The id-path, from an instance id of THIS scene inward ($root addresses a composable sub-scene root).
JSON pointer into the addressed entity (field override; paired with `value`). /id, /$schema and /version are immutable under composition (L-37).
The overriding value — any JSON (paired with `pointer`).

Optional divergence snapshot `context set` records with a field override (R-CLI-006 / L-35): the template value at write time. `context query --overrides diverged` flags an override whose `base` no longer matches the current template value (advisory; never auto-pruned).
Structural override: an entity object (with its own stable id) composed under the instance subtree `path` addresses.
Structural override: remove the entity (or whole instance subtree) `path` addresses.
Schema-blessed annotations.

The scene-root entity (L-35): scene-level state as singleton components. Inert by default when this scene is instanced as a sub-scene; `composable: true` opts it into the parent flatten. Bakes are derived artifacts, never authored.
Optional stable intra-file id; when absent the root is addressable in override paths as `$root`.
Opt-in: compose this root's components when the scene is instanced as a sub-scene (default false = inert).
Singleton scene-level components.
Schema-blessed annotations.
The scene's entities — an id-keyed stable collection: an array of objects each carrying a stable intra-file `id` (L-33; the map-keyed form is forbidden, R-FILE-001).
Stable intra-file id: collision-resistant random lowercase hex, >= 64-bit, file-scoped, never sequential (L-33). Required for composition — an id-less entity cannot carry composed identity.
Schema-blessed annotations at the entity level.
Schema-blessed annotations at the component-map level.
Schema-blessed annotations.
World position, meters (SI units law).
Schema-blessed annotations.
Vertical field of view, RADIANS (the units law: no degrees in authored data).

schema.description: An authored scene (L-32 canonical JSON; L-33 one scene per file).

entities.description: The scene's entities — an id-keyed stable collection: an array of objects each carrying a stable intra-file `id` (L-33; the map-keyed form is forbidden, R-FILE-001).

fov.description: Vertical field of view, RADIANS (the units law: no degrees in authored data).

notes.description: Schema-blessed annotations at the component-map level.

id.description: Stable intra-file id: collision-resistant random lowercase hex, >= 64-bit, file-scoped, never sequential (L-33). Required for composition — an id-less entity cannot carry composed identity.

notes.description: Schema-blessed annotations at the entity level.

instances.description: Scene composition (L-35): each entry instances another scene under a stable intra-file id; per-instance state lives in `overrides`.

id.description: Stable intra-file id of this instance — the first segment of override id-paths addressing into its subtree (L-33/L-35).

scene.description: Project-root-relative path of the instanced scene (becomes a typed x-ctx-ref when the asset database lands, L-34/L-36).

kind.description: The M1 placeholder kind marker; the $schema header is authoritative.

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

overrides.description: Id-path-addressed override entries ([instanceId, ..., entityId] — L-35), innermost-out precedence (the OUTERMOST instancing scene wins). Exactly one kind per entry: `pointer`+`value` (per-field), `add` (structural add), or `remove: true` (structural remove) — enforced by the composition model.

add.description: Structural override: an entity object (with its own stable id) composed under the instance subtree `path` addresses.

base.description: Optional divergence snapshot `context set` records with a field override (R-CLI-006 / L-35): the template value at write time. `context query --overrides diverged` flags an override whose `base` no longer matches the current template value (advisory; never auto-pruned).

path.description: The id-path, from an instance id of THIS scene inward ($root addresses a composable sub-scene root).

pointer.description: JSON pointer into the addressed entity (field override; paired with `value`). /id, /$schema and /version are immutable under composition (L-37).

remove.description: Structural override: remove the entity (or whole instance subtree) `path` addresses.

value.description: The overriding value — any JSON (paired with `pointer`).

root.description: The scene-root entity (L-35): scene-level state as singleton components. Inert by default when this scene is instanced as a sub-scene; `composable: true` opts it into the parent flatten. Bakes are derived artifacts, never authored.

composable.description: Opt-in: compose this root's components when the scene is instanced as a sub-scene (default false = inert).

id.description: Optional stable intra-file id; when absent the root is addressable in override paths as `$root`.

fields.description: Project-relative path of the startup scene (becomes a typed x-ctx-ref when the asset database lands, L-34/L-36).
Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

schema.description: The project manifest `context new` scaffolds (R-QA-006 runnable template).

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

scene.description: Project-relative path of the startup scene (becomes a typed x-ctx-ref when the asset database lands, L-34/L-36).

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
World size of one cell, meters (SI units law): [width, height].
Optional overall grid extent, in CELLS (dimensionless counts).
Schema-blessed annotations.
Grid width in cells.
Grid height in cells.
The atlases tiles are painted from — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array of objects, never a map).
Schema-blessed annotations.
Stable intra-file id: collision-resistant random lowercase hex, >= 64-bit, file-scoped, never sequential (L-33).
Human-readable tile-set name.
Typed reference to the atlas asset (L-34 dual form); target-kind enforced through the asset-db meta lookup once an atlas kind lands (L-36 / R-DATA-006).
Global tile-id of this set's first tile; per-set id ranges never overlap (Tiled-style offset).
Tiles in the atlas; the set spans global ids [firstTileId, firstTileId + tileCount).
Draw layers, back-to-front — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001). Each layer's cells are chunked; each chunk's packed tile-id grid lives in a binary sidecar.
Schema-blessed annotations.
Stable intra-file id (L-33).
Layer opacity in [0, 1] (dimensionless).
Whether the layer is drawn (default true).
Cell-grid regions. The heavy per-cell tile-id data lives OUT of the JSON in a binary sidecar (L-33), sized under the ~1 MB split-nudge ceiling.
Schema-blessed annotations.
Chunk bounds in CELL coordinates: [x, y, width, height] (dimensionless).
Binary sidecar of the packed per-cell tile ids (width*height u32 values), sized under the split-nudge ceiling (L-33).

schema.description: A 2D tilemap (R-2D-003, L-32/L-33): back-to-front layers of chunked cell regions whose heavy per-cell tile-id grids live in binary sidecars, painted from atlas tile-sets.

grid.description: Optional overall grid extent, in CELLS (dimensionless counts).

layers.description: Draw layers, back-to-front — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001). Each layer's cells are chunked; each chunk's packed tile-id grid lives in a binary sidecar.

chunks.description: Cell-grid regions. The heavy per-cell tile-id data lives OUT of the JSON in a binary sidecar (L-33), sized under the ~1 MB split-nudge ceiling.

cells.description: Binary sidecar of the packed per-cell tile ids (width*height u32 values), sized under the split-nudge ceiling (L-33).

region.description: Chunk bounds in CELL coordinates: [x, y, width, height] (dimensionless).

visible.description: Whether the layer is drawn (default true).

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

tileSets.description: The atlases tiles are painted from — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array of objects, never a map).

atlas.description: Typed reference to the atlas asset (L-34 dual form); target-kind enforced through the asset-db meta lookup once an atlas kind lands (L-36 / R-DATA-006).

firstTileId.description: Global tile-id of this set's first tile; per-set id ranges never overlap (Tiled-style offset).

id.description: Stable intra-file id: collision-resistant random lowercase hex, >= 64-bit, file-scoped, never sequential (L-33).

tileCount.description: Tiles in the atlas; the set spans global ids [firstTileId, firstTileId + tileCount).

tileSize.description: World size of one cell, meters (SI units law): [width, height].

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
BCP-47 tag of the authoritative source locale (the implicit final fallback for every key).
Declared locales + their fallback chains — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `locale`, never a map).
Schema-blessed annotations.
BCP-47 locale tag (e.g. "en", "pt-BR") — the stable key.
Human-readable locale name.
Ordered fallback chain: locale tags tried, in order, when a key has no value for this locale.
The string entries — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `key`). Downstream systems bind these keys.
Schema-blessed annotations.
The stable string key downstream systems bind (the message id).
Per-locale translations — an id-keyed stable collection keyed by `locale`. A value is EXACTLY ONE of `text` (simple) or `plural` (CLDR categories).
Schema-blessed annotations.
The locale this translation targets.
The simple (non-plural) translation.
CLDR plural forms; `other` is the required catch-all. The engine selects a category by locale + count (R-I18N-001 ICU-style rules).
Schema-blessed annotations.

schema.description: A localization string table (R-I18N-001, L-32): locale variants, fallback chains, and ICU/CLDR plural rules. Downstream systems bind string KEYS from day one.

keys.description: The string entries — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `key`). Downstream systems bind these keys.

key.description: The stable string key downstream systems bind (the message id).

values.description: Per-locale translations — an id-keyed stable collection keyed by `locale`. A value is EXACTLY ONE of `text` (simple) or `plural` (CLDR categories).

plural.description: CLDR plural forms; `other` is the required catch-all. The engine selects a category by locale + count (R-I18N-001 ICU-style rules).

locales.description: Declared locales + their fallback chains — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `locale`, never a map).

fallback.description: Ordered fallback chain: locale tags tried, in order, when a key has no value for this locale.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale tag (e.g. "en", "pt-BR") — the stable key.

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

sourceLocale.description: BCP-47 tag of the authoritative source locale (the implicit final fallback for every key).

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
The deterministic PRNG seed the run started from (splitmix64).
The number of fixed ticks the run advanced (R-SIM-002).
The named headless scenario the session ran (the built-in default is `demo`).
The engine version that recorded the artifact (semver).
The contract protocol major the artifact was recorded under (frozen at 1 by the M3 contract freeze, R-CLI-004).
Whether the run is deterministically reproducible; a non-deterministic artifact carries no expected trace and replays best-effort.

The project inputs the run ran against + their canonical content hashes at record time. Replay verifies these BEFORE running so a replay against drifted content is reported as drift, never a silent divergence.
Project-root-relative path of the input file.
The serializer canonical-content hash (R-FILE-001) of the file's bytes at record time.
The recorded per-tick input: synthetic input events + mapped action activations, timestamped to the tick they applied at. Replay feeds these back at the same ticks (R-QA-005 record/replay).
The sim tick this input applied at.
Raw synthetic input events (the low-level layer).
The input device (e.g. `key`, `mouse`).
The code on the device (e.g. `W`).
The integer value of the event.
Mapped action activations (the gameplay/UI layer).
The mapped action name (e.g. `move_x`, `ui_submit`).
The action lifecycle phase.
The integer value of the activation.
The expected per-tick canonical root-hash trace (index i == tick i), present only in deterministic mode. Replay compares each tick's actual root against this and reports the first divergent tick.

schema.description: A versioned, recorded headless run (R-QA-005, L-54): seed + input stream + tick count + engine/protocol versions + a content-hash manifest of the project inputs + (deterministic mode) the expected per-tick root-hash trace. Replay verifies the manifest before running (drift is reported, never silent divergence) and reports the first-divergence tick; non-deterministic replay is labeled best-effort.

contentManifest.description: The project inputs the run ran against + their canonical content hashes at record time. Replay verifies these BEFORE running so a replay against drifted content is reported as drift, never a silent divergence.

hash.description: The serializer canonical-content hash (R-FILE-001) of the file's bytes at record time.

path.description: Project-root-relative path of the input file.

deterministic.description: Whether the run is deterministically reproducible; a non-deterministic artifact carries no expected trace and replays best-effort.

engineVersion.description: The engine version that recorded the artifact (semver).

expectedHashTrace.description: The expected per-tick canonical root-hash trace (index i == tick i), present only in deterministic mode. Replay compares each tick's actual root against this and reports the first divergent tick.

inputStream.description: The recorded per-tick input: synthetic input events + mapped action activations, timestamped to the tick they applied at. Replay feeds these back at the same ticks (R-QA-005 record/replay).

value.description: The integer value of the activation.

events.description: Raw synthetic input events (the low-level layer).

value.description: The integer value of the event.

tick.description: The sim tick this input applied at.

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

protocolMajor.description: The contract protocol major the artifact was recorded under (frozen at 1 by the M3 contract freeze, R-CLI-004).

scenario.description: The named headless scenario the session ran (the built-in default is `demo`).

seed.description: The deterministic PRNG seed the run started from (splitmix64).

tickCount.description: The number of fixed ticks the run advanced (R-SIM-002).

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
The id of the state the graph starts in (must name a declared state).
The graph's states. Each plays one clip and lists its outgoing parameter-gated transitions, checked in order (first satisfied wins).
Stable state id, unique within the graph.
The DCC-imported clip this state plays.
Outgoing transitions, evaluated in authored order; the first whose condition holds fires.
Target state id (must name a declared state).
The control-parameter comparison: >=, >, <=, <.
The control-parameter threshold the comparison is against.
The cross-fade duration in seconds when this transition fires (>= 0; 0 is instant).

schema.description: An authored animation state-machine / transition graph (R-SYS-008): named states, each playing one DCC-imported clip, connected by transitions gated on an integer control parameter. Canonical JSON (L-32) evaluated deterministically by the animation package (src/packages/animation/) — no in-engine clip authoring (R-ASSET-001). Referential integrity (the initial state + every transition target + parameter references resolve) is checked by the kind's semantic analyzer beyond this shape.

initial.description: The id of the state the graph starts in (must name a declared state).

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

states.description: The graph's states. Each plays one clip and lists its outgoing parameter-gated transitions, checked in order (first satisfied wins).

clip.description: The DCC-imported clip this state plays.

id.description: Stable state id, unique within the graph.

transitions.description: Outgoing transitions, evaluated in authored order; the first whose condition holds fires.

duration.description: The cross-fade duration in seconds when this transition fires (>= 0; 0 is instant).

threshold.description: The control-parameter threshold the comparison is against.

to.description: Target state id (must name a declared state).

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
The mixing buses — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `id`, never a map). Each bus scales the sound routed to it and folds into its parent bus.
Schema-blessed annotations.
Stable intra-file bus id, unique within the graph (the routing key audio events name).
Human-readable bus name.
Linear gain multiplier applied to this bus (dimensionless; >= 0, 1.0 is unity).
Optional id of the bus this bus folds into; absent means a top-level (master) bus. Must name a declared bus and form no cycle (checked by the kind semantics).

schema.description: An authored audio mixing-bus graph (R-SYS-006, L-32/L-46): named buses with a linear gain and an optional parent bus, forming the mix tree the presentation-observer audio package routes sound events through. Bus-id uniqueness, parent resolution, and parent-acyclicity are checked by the kind's semantic analyzer beyond this shape.

buses.description: The mixing buses — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `id`, never a map). Each bus scales the sound routed to it and folds into its parent bus.

gain.description: Linear gain multiplier applied to this bus (dimensionless; >= 0, 1.0 is unity).

id.description: Stable intra-file bus id, unique within the graph (the routing key audio events name).

parent.description: Optional id of the bus this bus folds into; absent means a top-level (master) bus. Must name a declared bus and form no cycle (checked by the kind semantics).

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).
The DCC-imported audio clip this event plays (R-ASSET-001 — no in-engine audio authoring).
The id of the mixing bus (in an authored ctx:audio-bus graph) this event routes to.
Linear gain applied when this event plays (dimensionless; >= 0, 1.0 is unity).
Whether the event loops until explicitly stopped (default false).
Optional 3D spatialization: when present the event is positioned in the world and attenuated by listener distance. Absent means a non-spatial (2D/UI) sound at full gain.
Schema-blessed annotations.
Distance, meters (SI units law), within which the event plays at full `gain` (no attenuation).
Distance, meters (SI units law), beyond which the event is fully attenuated to silence; must exceed minDistance (checked by the kind semantics).

schema.description: An authored sound event (R-SYS-006, L-32/L-46): a DCC-imported clip routed to a mixing bus with a linear gain and an optional 3D spatialization block. Triggered by the presentation-observer audio package (src/packages/audio/) off the deterministic sim path (R-SIM-001). The attenuation-range consistency is checked by the kind's semantic analyzer beyond this shape.

bus.description: The id of the mixing bus (in an authored ctx:audio-bus graph) this event routes to.

clip.description: The DCC-imported audio clip this event plays (R-ASSET-001 — no in-engine audio authoring).

gain.description: Linear gain applied when this event plays (dimensionless; >= 0, 1.0 is unity).

loop.description: Whether the event loops until explicitly stopped (default false).

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

spatial.description: Optional 3D spatialization: when present the event is positioned in the world and attenuated by listener distance. Absent means a non-spatial (2D/UI) sound at full gain.

maxDistance.description: Distance, meters (SI units law), beyond which the event is fully attenuated to silence; must exceed minDistance (checked by the kind semantics).

minDistance.description: Distance, meters (SI units law), within which the event plays at full `gain` (no attenuation).

fields.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

The named input actions bindings map to — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `id`, never a map). Gameplay actions (e.g. move_x, fire) feed InputState's gameplay channels; ui_* actions feed its UI channel.
Schema-blessed annotations.
Stable action id, unique within the document (the name bindings target and the sim InputState sink understands).
The routing layer this action belongs to: gameplay (sim-affecting) or ui (arbitrated above gameplay). Advisory; defaults to gameplay.

The stackable input contexts (the L-45 layered UI-capture stack). Higher contexts take priority; a capturing UI context blocks unbound input from reaching gameplay contexts below it (R-SYS-007 focus arbitration).
Schema-blessed annotations.
Stable context id, unique within the document (the key the router pushes/pops).
The context's routing layer: gameplay or ui.

When true (a modal UI context), the context swallows any input it does not itself bind so gameplay contexts below never receive it (default false — a non-capturing overlay lets unbound input fall through).
The device->action bindings this context contributes.
Schema-blessed annotations.
The raw device source: keyboard | mouse | gamepad | touch | vr (VR-controller).
The device-specific code (e.g. keyboard "W", mouse "MouseLeft", gamepad "ButtonSouth").
The action this source maps to (must name a declared action — checked by the kind semantics).

schema.description: An authored input-bindings configuration (R-SYS-007, L-32/L-45): the named input actions plus the stackable input contexts (each on a gameplay/ui layer, optionally capturing) that map raw device sources to those actions. The routing front-end (src/packages/input/) applies the layered UI-capture arbitration and feeds the mapped actions to the sim InputState sink. Action-id / context-id uniqueness and every binding resolving to a declared action are checked by the kind's semantic analyzer beyond this shape.

actions.description: The named input actions bindings map to — an id-keyed stable collection (L-33 / R-FILE-001: array keyed by `id`, never a map). Gameplay actions (e.g. move_x, fire) feed InputState's gameplay channels; ui_* actions feed its UI channel.

id.description: Stable action id, unique within the document (the name bindings target and the sim InputState sink understands).

layer.description: The routing layer this action belongs to: gameplay (sim-affecting) or ui (arbitrated above gameplay). Advisory; defaults to gameplay.

contexts.description: The stackable input contexts (the L-45 layered UI-capture stack). Higher contexts take priority; a capturing UI context blocks unbound input from reaching gameplay contexts below it (R-SYS-007 focus arbitration).

bindings.description: The device->action bindings this context contributes.

action.description: The action this source maps to (must name a declared action — checked by the kind semantics).

code.description: The device-specific code (e.g. keyboard "W", mouse "MouseLeft", gamepad "ButtonSouth").

device.description: The raw device source: keyboard | mouse | gamepad | touch | vr (VR-controller).

capture.description: When true (a modal UI context), the context swallows any input it does not itself bind so gameplay contexts below never receive it (default false — a non-capturing overlay lets unbound input fall through).

id.description: Stable context id, unique within the document (the key the router pushes/pops).

layer.description: The context's routing layer: gameplay or ui.

notes.description: Schema-blessed human/AI annotations — string or array of strings (L-32 bans JSON comments).

deprecationPolicy.compatibilityWindow: negotiated capability subset; hard-fail outside the window; the negotiation/degradation behavior activates at the 2nd released protocol version (v1 is hard-fail-on-mismatch).
deprecationPolicy.note: Active at protocolMajor=1 (M3 contract freeze) — the frozen surface changes only through this deprecation lifecycle; a breaking change without a cycle is rejected (R-CLI-004).
